info.name: ColorTouch API
info.description: REST API for the ColorTouch SDK server: developer onboarding (base palette generation), end-user personalization, the canonical questionnaire, and dashboard analytics. Run the server locally first (`npm run dev` from server/, mock mode — no Groq calls) before using this collection.

variable.description: Must match a developerId that has already been onboarded (run "Onboard Developer" first).
Postman's dynamic variable generates a fresh UUID per request run.

request.description: 201 on success, returning the generated BasePalette. 400 if appMetadata fails schema validation; 503 if AI generation fails (live mode only).
200 with the developer's BasePalette (wrapped as a PaletteResponse), or 404 if onboarding hasn't run yet for this developerId.

200 with a PersonalizedPalette. Requires the 5 core questions at minimum (see GET /questions for the full set and valid answer options). 404 if developerId hasn't been onboarded; 503 if AI generation fails (live mode only).

Same as the plain request, but the response also echoes back the exact prompt sent to the AI provider under `debug.prompt` (see the Personalization Simulator / Prompt Tuning pages in the portal, which use this).
The canonical question set (5 core + 10 optional deep-dive), the single source of truth every client should render from.
Apps onboarded, total personalizations, unique users, personalization rate, retention, and current AI provider mode (mock/live).
One record per completed questionnaire for this developer: answers, resulting palette, and the AI's design rationale. Omit developerId to get every submission across all developers.

AI-generated analysis of who this developer's end users are and what their app provides, grounded in aggregated stats over their submissions. 400 if developerId is missing; 404 if that developer hasn't onboarded an app yet.
Every request this server process has handled recently (method, path, status, duration) — backs the portal's System Logs page.
